exit_conditions.value: END node is reached via N4 or N6
exit_conditions.source_text: N4 -> END [continuation not justified], N6 -> END

required_steps.value: N1: expands one state into multiple paths.
required_steps.source_text: N1 expansions / N1 -> N2A, N1 -> N2B, N1 -> N2C

required_steps.value: N4: re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.
required_steps.source_text: N4 re-sorts path fit after the new input appears.

optional_steps.value: N2A, N2B, N2C: intermediate parallel paths expanded by N1
optional_steps.ambiguity: These are structured as alternative parallel options or independent path steps, executed conditionally or jointly based on N1 expansion.

optional_steps.value: N5: reopens continuation when separation remains weak.
N6: closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

loops.value: Loop from N4 to N5 and back to N2A/N2B/N2C when threshold not met
loops.source_text:
N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]
N5 -> N2A
N5 -> N2B
N5 -> N2C

branches.value: Branch at N1 to N2A, N2B, or N2C
branches.source_text:
N1 -> N2A
N1 -> N2B
N1 -> N2C
branches.ambiguity: Ambiguous whether these are executed in parallel, non-deterministically, or represent distinct options evaluated simultaneously.

branches.value: Three-way conditional branch at N4 based on state evaluation
branches.source_text:
N4 -> N5 [threshold not met]
N4 -> N6 [one path selected]
N4 -> END [continuation not justified]

termination_conditions.value: One path remains strongest after re-sorting leading to N6 and END
termination_conditions.source_text:
N4 -> N6 [one path selected]
N6 -> END

structural_layer.control_flow_shape: Divergent-convergent paths with a feedback loop back to an intermediate layer and multiple exit points.

primitive_layer.dominant_primitive: Conditional branching and looping based on separation/sorting thresholds

preconditions.value: An initial single state exists to be expanded.
preconditions.source_text: N1 expands one state into multiple paths.
preconditions.ambiguity: Exact format of state is unspecified.

postconditions.value: The process terminates either with one strongest path selected or because continuation is not justified.
postconditions.source_text:
N4 -> END [continuation not justified]
N6 closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting.

stopping_criteria.value: One path remains strongest after re-sorting (reaching N6 then END)

body.description: expands one state into multiple paths
re-sorts path fit after the new input appears
reopens continuation when separation remains weak
closes the graph when one path remains strongest after re-sorting

representation_layer.natural_language_summary: The procedure begins at START and enters N1, where a single state is expanded into three parallel paths (N2A, N2B, N2C). These paths converge at N3, which introduces a differentiating input. At N4, the path fit is re-sorted based on this new input. From N4, one of three conditional branches is taken: if the threshold is not met, execution moves to N5, which reopens continuation due to weak separation and loops back to the paths N2A/N2B/N2C; if one path is selected, it goes to N6 and terminates at END; if continuation is not justified, it goes straight to END.
representation_layer.canonical_summary: START -> N1 -> [N2A, N2B, N2C] -> N3 -> N4 -> {if threshold not met -> N5 -> [N2A, N2B, N2C]; if one path selected -> N6 -> END; if continuation not justified -> END}

ambiguities.description: The precise execution mode of multiple paths (N2A, N2B, N2C) is not explicitly defined.
plausible_interpretations: Parallel processing of all three paths simultaneously.
Sequential evaluation of each path alternative.
ambiguities.effect_on_extraction: Affects the modeling of concurrency vs. single-path branching in the AST and structural representation.

notes: The extraction is derived entirely from the provided node connectivity graph and explicit functional notes for N1, N3, N4, N5, and N6.
